labels.component_1: The Quantum Engine (Layers 1-2): We begin with the Quantum Biological (Layer 1) substrate, where biological systems create order from quantum potentiality, stabilised by mechanisms like QEC and neuroimmune quantum effects. This quantum information is then transduced by the Neurochemical-Neurological (Layer 2) layer, which acts as a dynamic filter, translating field intent into the biochemical language of the nervous system.
labels.component_2: The Quantum-Classical Bridge: Selection and Amplification
labels.component_3: I. Guided Einselection (The Emergence of Classicality)
labels.component_4: II. The Amplification Mechanism: Quantum Stochastic Resonance (QSR) at Criticality
